Campaign Frame — 01Concept Study 01, Petal Light
Concept Study 01

Color Cosmetics Campaign

A visual exploration by UNCAST.CO

CategoryBeauty, Color Cosmetics
ScopeCampaign Concept, Art Direction, Production
DeliverablesHero Visual, Social Campaign Set

The Brief

An iridescent lip gloss launch, imagined as a campaign that feels closer to a fine-fragrance release than a mass-market beauty ad — soft, luminous, botanical.

The Concept

We built "Petal Light" — a world of glass-skin, flushed cheek and translucent lilac, the product held like a flower rather than displayed like a product shot.

Art Direction

Warm-white studio light, dewy skin finish, a single recurring flower motif tying every frame back to the shade's name. Nothing overworked, nothing oversaturated.

Moodboard

Reference pull from fine-fragrance and floral photography, distilled into one consistent light quality.

AI Production

Direction trained across skin finish, gloss texture and floral styling, generated and graded frame by frame.

Result

A full campaign visual set delivered in days, held to the same finish as a physical beauty shoot.

Concept Study 02

Skincare Campaign

A visual exploration by UNCAST.CO

CategorySkincare, Serum
ScopeCreative Direction, Visual System
DeliverablesHero Campaign, Product Detail Set

The Brief

A hero serum campaign, imagined to read as clinical and premium at once — proof over promise, without losing the warmth of a beauty editorial.

The Concept

"Bare Light" — damp skin, hard directional sun, the bottle held close to the face so the product and the result share the same frame.

Art Direction

Close, unretouched-feeling skin texture, warm-neutral grading, sunlight used as the campaign's signature rather than a studio softbox.

Moodboard

Skin-texture and sunlit-portrait references paired for dermatological accuracy.

AI Production

Skin-accurate generation pipeline refined across dozens of iterations for realism.

Result

A hero campaign and detail set delivered in place of a multi-day location shoot.

Campaign Frame — 02Concept Study 02, Bare Light
Campaign Frame — 03Concept Study 03, Golden Hour
Concept Study 03

Fragrance Campaign

A visual exploration by UNCAST.CO

CategoryFragrance, Luxury
ScopeCampaign Concept, Art Direction, Production
DeliverablesHero Visual, Print Campaign

The Brief

A fragrance launch visual, imagined with the gravity of an old-house campaign, built for a name entering the market for the first time.

The Concept

"Golden Hour" — the bottle caught mid-spray against a city at dusk, gold dust suspended in the air, the scent felt rather than seen.

Art Direction

Warm backlight, soft bokeh, gold particulate as a recurring texture across the whole campaign — restraint used as the luxury signal.

Moodboard

Golden-hour cityscape and vintage perfume advertising as the two core references.

AI Production

Custom lighting model trained to hold consistent backlight and particulate across every frame.

Result

A hero and print campaign built without a single physical studio day.

Concept Study 04

Prestige Skincare Campaign

A visual exploration by UNCAST.CO

CategorySkincare, Prestige
ScopeCampaign Concept, Art Direction
DeliverablesHero Film Stills, Print Campaign

The Brief

A power-serum relaunch needed a campaign with real drama — energy, heat, motion — for a category usually shot flat and clinical.

The Concept

"Crimson Energy" — silk in motion, hard rim light, the bottle held like a flare rather than presented on a shelf. Movement standing in for potency.

Art Direction

Saturated crimson field, single hard backlight, fabric used to generate motion in an otherwise static product shot.

Moodboard

Motion photography and richly saturated color-field references as the starting direction.

AI Production

Custom motion and lighting model trained to hold consistent drape physics across every frame.

Result

A print and hero campaign built without a single physical studio day.

Campaign Frame — 04Concept Study 04, Crimson Energy

Services

Three disciplines, one studio. From first concept to the final delivered asset.

  • Creative Direction

    Campaign concepts, visual strategy and art direction — defining how a brand looks and feels before a single frame is produced.

    Strategy & Concept

  • AI Campaign Production

    Beauty campaigns, product worlds and editorial imagery — produced through a proprietary AI workflow, directed to the same standard as a physical set.

    Production

  • Visual Content Systems

    Social media assets, launch campaigns and brand consistency — a visual language built to extend across every channel a brand shows up in.

    Content & Systems

1Creative Director, Every Project
The Studio Position

AI does not replace creativity.
It removes production limitations.

Human Taste

Every direction, every frame, every grade is a decision made by a person who has led campaigns before — not a prompt left to chance.

Human Decisions

AI proposes options at a scale no set ever could. A creative director still chooses which one is right.

Faster Execution

Weeks of production compress into days, without compressing the standard the work is held to.

Unlimited Exploration

A hundred versions of a single frame cost nothing but time — so the right one gets found, not settled for.

Questions

What clients usually ask before starting.

  • Is this really AI, or retouched photography?

    Every frame is AI-generated and directed, start to finish — no physical shoot, no stock imagery. Direction, styling and grading are done by hand, the production is AI.

  • Will it look like a template, or match my brand?

    Direction is built around your brand's specific world — color, texture, mood, product — not a reused style pulled off the shelf.

  • How fast is delivery?

    Single campaigns typically ship in 5–10 days depending on scope. Brand Systems and retainers run on a schedule we set together at kickoff.

  • Can I request revisions?

    Yes — unlimited exploration is part of the process, not a paid add-on. We iterate until the direction is right.

  • What if I'm not sure which package fits?

    Send a brief or book a call — we'll look at your brand and recommend the right scope before anything is agreed.
